title: Ocean Zones Interactive
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive p5.js vertical cross-section of the ocean from surface to 6000m showing physical conditions and representative organisms at each depth zone

learningObjectives: Describe the physical conditions and representative organisms of each ocean zone
Explain how light, temperature, and pressure gradients determine marine life distribution
Compare adaptations of organisms living at different ocean depths
prerequisites: Conceptual knowledge of light, temperature, and pressure changes with depth

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive depth exploration with real-time data display
keyQuestions: Why is the epipelagic zone the most biologically productive ocean layer?
How do organisms in the deep ocean obtain energy without sunlight?
What adaptations allow organisms to survive the extreme pressure of the abyssal zone?
How does the thermocline affect nutrient mixing between ocean layers?
commonMisconceptions: The deep ocean is lifeless -- in fact, diverse communities thrive around hydrothermal vents and on the ocean floor
Ocean temperature decreases steadily with depth -- most temperature change occurs in the thermocline, with deep water being relatively uniform
Light penetrates much deeper than it actually does -- the photic zone extends only about 200m, less than 5% of average ocean depth
assessmentOpportunities: Have students graph temperature, light, and pressure versus depth and identify non-linear patterns
Ask students to explain why a specific organism cannot survive outside its zone
Compare student pre-exploration sketches with actual conditions revealed by the simulation
